name: F2
notes: F2 Venture Capital is an Israeli early-stage VC firm headquartered in Tel Aviv, focused on cloud infrastructure and applied AI for Israeli startups. Domain f2vc.com confirmed.

name: Field Ventures
notes: Field Ventures (New York), founded 2025 by Richard Kerby and Jillian Williams. Multiple firms share this name; most active current entity is the NY-based one at fieldvc.com.

name: Floodgate
notes: Floodgate Fund, founded 2006 by Mike Maples Jr. and Ann Miura-Ko. HQ at 506 Santa Cruz Ave, Suite 200, Menlo Park, CA 94025.

name: Formation VC
notes: Formation (New York) is a pre-seed/seed VC founded 2024. Note: Formation 8 (SF, now defunct) is a separate entity. The active 'Formation VC' entity appears to be the NY fund at formation.ventures; slight ambiguity remains.

name: Founders Circle
notes: Founders Circle Capital, HQ San Francisco, CA. Secondary office in Campbell, CA.

name: Frank Ahmed
notes: Frank Ahmed appears to be an individual person (Managing Partner at Vision Capital LP), not a company. Cannot reliably enrich as a company record — no standalone company website or HQ to identify.

name: G9 Ventures
notes: G9 Ventures, founded 2017, invests in consumer health/wellness brands. HQ New York City, NY.

name: GGV
notes: GGV Capital, historically HQ in Menlo Park, CA. Split in March 2024 into Granite Asia (Singapore) and Notable Capital (US). The legacy GGV entity was US-based in Menlo Park.

name: Global Founders Capital
notes: Global Founders Capital (GFC) is headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Has global offices including SF and NYC but HQ is Berlin.

name: Google Ventures
notes: Google Ventures (now branded as GV) is the venture capital arm of Alphabet, HQ Mountain View, CA. Official site is gv.com.

name: Green Oak
notes: Best match for 'Green Oak' (two words) is GreenOak Real Estate, HQ at 285 Madison Ave, New York, NY. Note: Greenoaks Capital Partners (one word, SF-based VC) is a different firm. Ambiguity exists given no domain in input.

name: Hf0
notes: HF0 Residency, a startup accelerator/VC founded 2023 by Dave Fontenot. 12-week residency program for repeat founders based in San Francisco, CA.
